events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess hazard: neutrals & grounds mixed in subpanel. In subpanel (separately derived system), neutral and ground must be isolated; neutral bus not bonded to enclosure; grounds bonded to enclosure. Mixing neutrals and grounds on same bus in subpanel creates objectionable current on grounding conductors, shock hazard, violates NEC 408.3? Actually 250.24(A)(5) maybe. Also 200.4 neutral conductors. 408.3 requires panelboard neutral bus insulated from enclosure unless used as service equipment. 250.142(B) for feeders. Severity high? It is a shock/electrocution hazard, fire. Immediate control: stop work, de-energize, separate neutrals and grounds, install isolated neutral bus, bond ground bus to enclosure, verify continuity, test, inspect. Decision fail. Risk high or critical? In residential subpanel, mixing neutrals and grounds can energize grounding conductors and enclosures; severity high. Could be critical if energized and accessible. But immediate controls required. Let's set risk high. Work stage in-progress. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 70. Findings: neutrals and equipment grounding conductors terminated on same bus in subpanel; neutral-ground bond present downstream of service disconnect; objectionable current on EGCs; enclosure may become energized. Actions: stop work, de-energize circuit, remove all neutral and ground conductors from shared bus, install separate isolated neutral bus and ground bus bonded to enclosure, terminate neutrals only on neutral bus, grounds only on ground bus, remove any neutral-ground bonding screw/strap in subpanel, verify proper isolation, torque terminations, re-energize and test. Rationale. References: NEC 408.3, 250.24(A)(5), 250.142(B), 200.4, 110.26 maybe working clearance not directly observed but relevant. Include 408.3(B) and 250.142(B). Need valid JSON. Ensure no prose outside.

  "decision": "fail",

  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 70,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Neutral conductors and equipment grounding conductors are terminated on the same bus in a subpanel.",
    "The subpanel has an improper neutral-to-ground bond downstream of the service disconnect.",
    "This creates objectionable current on grounding conductors and can energize the panel enclosure and downstream equipment.",
    "Condition violates the required isolation of grounded and grounding conductors in a separately derived/sub-fed panelboard."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work and de-energize the subpanel before correcting terminations.",
    "Remove all neutral and equipment grounding conductors from the shared bus.",
    "Install a separate, isolated neutral bus and a separate ground bus bonded to the panel enclosure.",
    "Terminate grounded (neutral) conductors only on the isolated neutral bus.",
    "Terminate equipment grounding conductors only on the ground bus bonded to the enclosure.",
    "Remove or ensure no neutral-to-ground bonding screw/strap is installed in the subpanel.",
    "Verify proper isolation, torque all terminations to manufacturer values, and re-energize only after testing and inspection."
  ],
  "rationale": "In a subpanel, neutrals must be isolated from the enclosure and from ground, while grounding conductors must be bonded to the enclosure. Mixing them creates a parallel current path on grounding conductors, shock and fire hazards, and violates NEC requirements for panelboard construction and grounding.",
  "references": [
    "NEC Art. 408",
    "NEC 408.3",
    "NEC 250.142(B)",
    "NEC 250.24(A)(5)",
    "NEC 200.4",
    "NEC 110.26"
  ]
}
